skill.name: comprehensive-code-review
skill.description: Guides a rigorous multi-pass review of pull requests and code changes, including context gathering, issue coverage, architecture checks, verification commands, and GitHub PR comment posting.
skill.summary: Run structured multi-pass code reviews for pull requests and local changes.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, and blocker patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown examples, Mermaid diagrams, and review terminology in SKILL.md. The confirmed concern is operational: the skill strongly mandates GitHub PR posting and includes gh and git commands, which can write public or private repository comments if followed without explicit user confirmation.

medium_findings.title: Mandatory external GitHub posting
medium_findings.description: The skill states that every review must be posted to GitHub and gives a gh pr comment command. This is a legitimate PR workflow, but it can cause unintended external writes if the agent follows it without explicit user consent. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The behavior is repeated as a mandatory requirement at multiple lines and includes a concrete posting command; the risk is operational rather than malicious.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is repeated as a mandatory requirement at multiple lines and includes a concrete posting command. The risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: External command examples are mostly benign review tooling
low_findings.description: The static command findings are primarily fenced examples for mkdir, git diff, gh pr view, gh pr diff, and gh pr comment. These commands match the stated code review purpose and do not show credential theft, destructive local changes, or obfuscation. Confidence: 0.91. Confidence reasoning: The relevant lines are Markdown workflow examples with standard git and GitHub CLI commands; no hidden executable script file exists in the reported file structure.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are Markdown workflow examples with standard git and GitHub CLI commands. No hidden executable script file exists in the reported file structure.

low_findings.title: Static blocker hits are false positives from review text
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM, weak crypto, and C2-style hits come from words such as CRITICAL, issue-closing keywords, and a Mermaid node named C2. No evidence found of credential access, weak cryptographic implementation, or command-and-control behavior. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: Targeted line review shows documentation and diagram syntax rather than executable malicious code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted line review shows documentation and diagram syntax rather than executable malicious code. The suspicious terms are part of normal review instructions or examples.

low_findings.title: Parallel reviewer mode may increase local writes and resource use
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to create a timestamped .reviews directory and launch multiple reviewers when a multiplier is requested. This can create local files and consume extra agent resources, but it is visible and aligned with the review workflow. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The instructions explicitly describe output directories and N reviewers, but they require user invocation of parallel mode.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly describe output directories and N reviewers. The behavior requires user invocation of parallel mode.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unconditional external publication instruction
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill repeatedly says posting to GitHub is mandatory and that local-only reviews fail. Marketplace users should be warned that this workflow writes review content to a PR. Confidence: 0.9. Confidence reasoning: The instruction is explicit, repeated, and paired with a concrete gh pr comment command.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit, repeated, and paired with a concrete gh pr comment command.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell command substitution in documented workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell command substitution for timestamped directories and posting generated body text. This is not malicious in context, but it is a pattern that should require normal command review. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The command substitution appears in fenced shell examples and uses local date and cat output, not untrusted network input.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution appears in fenced shell examples. It uses local date and cat output, not untrusted network input.

content.value_statement: Code reviews can miss runtime failures, inconsistent patterns, and incomplete requirements. This skill provides a structured multi-pass review workflow for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Guides a six-pass review for technical issues, consistency, architecture, environment fit, verification, and synthesis.
Collects pull request context with GitHub CLI and git diff commands.
Supports parallel reviewer mode for consensus-based review synthesis.
Produces structured sections for change explanation, findings, questions, checks, and task summary.
Checks issue linking, requirement coverage, changed files, and verification strategy.
Provides a workflow for posting the final review as a GitHub PR comment.
limitations: Requires repository access and GitHub CLI setup for PR workflows.
Mandatory posting instructions may not fit local-only or private review processes.
Review quality depends on available code context and model judgment.
Parallel mode can use more time, tokens, and local workspace files.

use_cases.title: Pre-merge pull request review
use_cases.description: Review a feature branch before merge and identify blocking defects, missing tests, and risky changes.

use_cases.title: Architecture and consistency audit
use_cases.description: Compare changed files against existing patterns and find duplication, coupling, and environment risks.

use_cases.title: Release readiness verification
use_cases.description: Generate specific checks and review summaries before approving production-facing changes.

prompt_templates.title: Review one PR
prompt_templates.prompt: Review PR 42 with the comprehensive code review workflow. Identify blocking issues, simplifications, questions, suggested checks, and a task summary.

prompt_templates.title: Review local changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current branch against main. Use the six-pass review method and focus on runtime failures, consistency, architecture, and verification commands.

prompt_templates.title: Check requirement coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request for full requirement coverage. Compare the PR description, related issues, changed files, and tests, then list any missing implementation work.

prompt_templates.title: Run parallel review synthesis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a 3X comprehensive code review for this PR. Synthesize convergent findings, divergent concerns, action items, and final merge guidance.

output_examples.output: A structured review comment with change explanation, blocking findings, simplification ideas, clarification questions, verification checks, and task summary.

output_examples.input: Review my branch for architecture risks.
output_examples.output: A review that highlights duplicated logic, inconsistent patterns, coupling risks, environment concerns, and practical refactoring options.

output_examples.input: Run a parallel review with three reviewers.
output_examples.output: A synthesized review that separates shared findings from single-reviewer concerns and prioritizes final action items.

best_practices: Confirm the PR number and posting permission before allowing any GitHub comment command.
Provide the PR description, linked issues, and test expectations for better review accuracy.
Run suggested verification commands before merging or approving changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to post public review comments without user approval.
Do not treat review findings as proof without checking the referenced code lines.
Do not enable parallel mode when a focused single-review pass is enough.

faq.question: Does this skill edit code?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for review, findings, and verification guidance, not implementation.

faq.question: Does it require GitHub?
faq.answer: GitHub is required for the documented PR posting workflow. Local review can still use git diff context.

faq.question: Can it review local changes?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes a path for reviewing local changes against the main branch.

faq.question: What is parallel review mode?
faq.answer: Parallel mode asks multiple independent reviewers to analyze the same changes and then combines their findings.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it strongly directs agents to post comments to GitHub and run external commands.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Engineers, technical leads, and release owners who need a rigorous review checklist before merge.
